refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plans and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that conflates productivity measurement with operational capability. Refinement separates the measurement/verification aspect from the capability delivery aspect, and explicitly acknowledges dual operator modes (on-site and tele-operated) as distinct execution contexts. This clarification is essential because productivity depends on both machine capability and operator effectiveness, which differ between direct and remote control.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trials under agreed conditions (soil type, task profile, weather, operator experience level). Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) and compare against productivity_target. Log operator mode (on-site vs. remote), task duration, material moved, and any safety/operational interruptions. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via incident/near-miss logs.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator control with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve productivity_target parity with on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce worksite safety policies and operational constraints without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (productivity metrics, machine state, operator mode) to Site Manager for monitoring and decision-making.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-power hydraulic drivetrain and bucket/arm kinematics optimized for cycle time and payload capacity; couple with operator interface (cab or remote) that minimizes control latency and cognitive load.

DP-B: Modular architecture with independent Vehicle subsystem (mechanical + local control), Teleoperation subsystem (network + remote UI), and Safety Services (policy enforcement layer) that coordinate via standardized interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert).

DP-C: Hybrid productivity model: Vehicle provides baseline mechanical throughput; Teleoperation adds remote capability with fallback to on-site; Safety Services acts as a constraint filter that permits tasks only if safety conditions are met, with real-time productivity impact logging.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B (Modular architecture with coordinated subsystems via standardized interfaces) with DP-C (Hybrid productivity model with constraint filtering).

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency directly affects operator cycle time and thus productivity; must be bounded and measured.
Safety Services constraints (e.g., geofencing, speed limits, load limits) reduce available operational envelope; must be tuned to preserve productivity_target.
Vehicle mechanical capability is independent of operator mode but operator effectiveness (on-site vs. remote) introduces coupling via situational awareness and control fidelity.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B decouples concerns via interfaces, reducing architectural coupling. DP-C acknowledges that productivity is a system-level emergent property dependent on all three subsystems. Coupling is managed through explicit interface contracts (Telemetry for monitoring, Tasking for command, SafetyAlert for constraint signaling) rather than tight integration.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology; risk: if target is unrealistic, no DP will satisfy it.
Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be kept below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip); risk: network infrastructure or remote site conditions may violate this.
Assumption: Safety constraints can be parameterized without hard-coded limits; risk: if safety policies are rigid, they may make remote operation infeasible.
Assumption: Operator training and worksite conditions are representative; risk: actual field conditions (soil variability, weather, operator skill) may differ from trial conditions.
Risk: High safety criticality means Safety Services must be fail-safe; if it fails, system must degrade gracefully without loss of productivity measurement capability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly define the interface contract (Telemetry schema, Tasking command set, SafetyAlert trigger conditions) and DP-C to quantify the productivity impact of typical safety constraints (e.g., 'geofencing reduces effective work area by X%, impacting productivity by Y%'). This will enable downstream DP refinement and risk quantification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The top-level system integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. Owns the Telemetry aggregation, Tasking dispatch, and SafetyAlert coordination logic. Responsible for end-to-end productivity measurement and reporting to Site Manager.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time stream of productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, payload), machine state (position, orientation, hydraulic pressure, fuel), operator mode (on-site/remote), and safety status. Consumed by Site Manager dashboard and Maintenance systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: High-level task commands from Site Manager or autonomous planner (e.g., 'excavate zone A', 'load truck B', 'return to depot'). Decomposed by NGHE Solution into Vehicle control commands and Teleoperation directives.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional safety signaling: Safety Services emits alerts (geofence breach, load limit exceeded, operator fatigue detected); NGHE Solution acknowledges and enforces constraints; Vehicle and Teleoperation report safety-relevant state (e.g., emergency stop status).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone for Teleoperation (remote operator commands, video/sensor feedback) and Telemetry uplink. Managed by Teleoperation subsystem; NGHE Solution monitors link quality and triggers fallback to on-site operation if latency exceeds threshold.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level integration point. It owns the productivity measurement logic, orchestrates subsystems, and interfaces with external stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance). Minimal diffusion: only the Telemetry aggregation logic and Tasking dispatch logic are contained here; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are separate modules.

Vehicle owns mechanical capability and local control, but productivity measurement requires data from Teleoperation (operator mode, latency) and Safety Services (constraint impact). Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse the productivity measurement responsibility across multiple modules.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and network link, but productivity depends on Vehicle capability and Safety Services constraints. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would ignore on-site operation mode and mechanical limitations.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement, but productivity is not a safety function; it is a business outcome. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would conflate safety with productivity and create inappropriate coupling.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner because: (1) Productivity is a system-level emergent property that depends on Vehicle capability, Teleoperation effectiveness, and Safety Services constraints; (2) NGHE Solution owns the integration logic that coordinates these subsystems and measures end-to-end productivity; (3) Assigning to any single subsystem would diffuse responsibility and create hidden dependencies.
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a 'god module' if it accumulates too much orchestration logic. Mitigation: Define clear, minimal orchestration responsibilities (Telemetry aggregation, Tasking dispatch, SafetyAlert coordination) and delegate subsystem-specific logic to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

Risk: Productivity measurement may require real-time data fusion from multiple subsystems; if data quality or synchronization is poor, measurement accuracy suffers. Mitigation: Define Telemetry schema with timestamps and data quality indicators; validate measurement logic against trial data.

Risk: Productivity target may be unachievable due to Safety Services constraints or Teleoperation latency; NGHE Solution must detect and report this condition. Mitigation: Implement productivity forecasting logic that predicts impact of constraints and alerts Site Manager if target is at risk.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine the NGHE Solution IM boundary to explicitly separate: (1) Telemetry aggregation (passive data collection and forwarding), (2) Tasking dispatch (command decomposition and routing), and (3) Productivity measurement (calculation of m³/h, ton/h, and comparison to target). Consider whether Productivity measurement should be a separate module (e.g., 'Productivity Monitor') or remain in NGHE Solution. Document the interface contracts (Telemetry schema, Tasking command set, SafetyAlert trigger conditions) to enable subsystem development in parallel.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (Modular architecture) and DP-C (Hybrid productivity model) directly address the refined FR by: (1) Enabling Vehicle to deliver mechanical capability, (2) Enabling Teleoperation to extend capability to remote operators, (3) Enabling Safety Services to enforce constraints without eliminating productivity, and (4) Enabling NGHE Solution to measure and report productivity. The DP is not over-constrained and does not introduce unnecessary complexity.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry aggregation logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, reducing diffusion. However, each subsystem (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) must emit Telemetry data; if schemas are inconsistent, aggregation becomes fragile. Mitigation: Define canonical Telemetry schema and enforce it via interface contracts.

Tasking dispatch logic is centralized in NGHE Solution, but Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services must each interpret and execute Tasking commands. If command semantics are ambiguous, execution may diverge. Mitigation: Define Tasking command set with clear semantics and validation rules.

SafetyAlert coordination is bidirectional; NGHE Solution must handle alerts from Safety Services and feedback from Vehicle/Teleoperation. If alert handling is ad-hoc, safety-critical conditions may be missed. Mitigation: Define SafetyAlert trigger conditions and response procedures; implement state machine for alert handling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate IM boundary for FR-NGHE-001 because it owns the integration logic that coordinates subsystems and measures productivity. The four sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) map cleanly to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively, with minimal diffusion. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and match the provided known interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of FR-NGHE-001 is feasible via representative operations trials: (1) Measure actual throughput (m³/h or ton/h) using Telemetry data; (2) Compare against productivity_target; (3) Log operator mode, task duration, material moved, and safety/operational interruptions; (4) Verify compliance with worksite safety plan. Acceptance criteria are quantitative and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution (top-level system integration). Sub-FRs are allocated to Vehicle (mechanical capability), Teleoperation (remote operation), Safety Services (constraint enforcement), and NGHE Solution (measurement and reporting). Allocation is clear and traceable. Each sub-FR is assigned to a single IM boundary, reducing ambiguity.

recommended_revisions: Refine the Telemetry interface to include a canonical schema for productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, payload, operator mode, safety status). This will enable consistent measurement across trials and subsystems.

Refine the Tasking interface to define a command set with clear semantics (e.g., 'excavate zone A' → Vehicle coordinates, depth, bucket angle; 'load truck B' → Vehicle position, truck ID; 'return to depot' → depot coordinates). This will enable unambiguous task execution.

Refine the SafetyAlert interface to define trigger conditions and response procedures (e.g., 'geofence breach' → alert severity, affected zone, recommended action). This will enable consistent safety enforcement.

Define a fallback strategy for Teleoperation latency: if Network Link latency exceeds threshold (e.g., 200 ms round-trip), automatically switch to on-site operation or pause task execution. This will preserve safety and productivity measurement integrity.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level acceptance criterion that requires decomposition into sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) to enable detailed design and verification. The refined FR and selected DP are sound, and the IM assignment to NGHE Solution is appropriate. However, the sub-FRs must be further decomposed to define the detailed design parameters for Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Additionally, the interface contracts (Telemetry schema, Tasking command set, SafetyAlert trigger conditions) must be refined to enable subsystem development in parallel. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to achieve sufficient design detail for implementation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The Vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, transport, dump) with mechanical capability to achieve productivity_target under nominal operating conditions.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The Teleoperation system shall enable remote operator control with latency, fidelity, and situational awareness sufficient to achieve productivity_target parity with on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The Safety Services system shall enforce worksite safety policies and operational constraints without reducing productivity below productivity_target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE Solution shall provide real-time Telemetry (productivity metrics, machine state, operator mode) to Site Manager for monitoring and decision-making.

revise.why: The selected DP (DP-B and DP-C) is sound at the system level, but must be refined to define detailed design parameters for each sub-FR. For example, DP-B must specify the interface contracts (Telemetry schema, Tasking command set, SafetyAlert trigger conditions) and DP-C must quantify the productivity impact of typical safety constraints. This refinement will enable subsystem-level DP selection and IM assignment.

assumptions: Productivity_target is achievable with current hydraulic and control technology.
Teleoperation latency can be kept below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip).
Safety constraints can be parameterized without hard-coded limits.
Operator training and worksite conditions are representative of actual field conditions.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and can be enforced via Safety Services.
Site Manager has access to real-time Telemetry data and can make informed decisions based on productivity metrics.
open_risks: Productivity_target may be unrealistic; if so, no DP will satisfy it. Mitigation: Validate target against historical data and expert judgment before proceeding.
Teleoperation latency may exceed operator perception threshold due to network infrastructure or remote site conditions. Mitigation: Conduct latency trials and define fallback strategy.
Safety constraints may be rigid and incompatible with productivity_target. Mitigation: Collaborate with Safety Manager to parameterize constraints and quantify productivity impact.
Operator skill and worksite conditions may differ from trial conditions, affecting actual productivity. Mitigation: Conduct trials under representative conditions and log variability.
Safety Services may fail or become unavailable, affecting system safety and productivity measurement. Mitigation: Define fail-safe behavior and implement redundancy if required by safety criticality.
evidence_needed: Historical productivity data from similar equipment and operations to validate productivity_target.
Network latency measurements from representative remote sites to validate Teleoperation feasibility.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies to define Safety Services constraints.
Operator training and skill assessment to validate representativeness of trials.
Mechanical capability data (hydraulic power, bucket capacity, cycle time) to validate Vehicle DP.
Telemetry schema and Tasking command set definitions to enable subsystem development.
Safety Services constraint parameterization and productivity impact quantification.
